title: Bill Payment Remittance Processing (BPRP)
body_text:
Bill Payment Remittance Processing (BPRP)
1	Overview of Bill Payment Remittance Processing (BPRP)
1.1	Overview of Bill Payment Remittance Processing (BPRP)
Bill Payment Remittance Processing (BPRP) is an electronic payment consolidation and settlement service financial institutions can offer their business members.
Business members (Corporate Creditors) are assigned a Corporate Creditor Identification Number (CCIN). The CCIN is used to consolidate electronic bill payments made by the business memberâs customers at participating financial institutions. If the business member does not have a CCIN, they apply for one as part of BPRP enrollment.
BPRP provides the following services:
Consolidates electronic bill payments made to the business memberâs CCIN.
Credits consolidated payment settlement to the business memberâs financial institution account.
Provides detailed payment reports for the business member for posting to its customersâ accounts.
Note:	Reports are created and distributed by your provincial central.
Provides notification of system changes and electronic bill payment outages.
Notifies the other financial institutions of the business customerâs BPRP service arrangements and CCIN.
For information on settlement and tracing:
BC and Ontario credit unions and other Central 1 clients, see Chapter 9, BPRP Settlement and Tracing.
Financial institutions in other provinces, contact your provincial central.
BC and Ontario credit unions and other Central 1 clients can request an optional data file to facilitate automated posting of bill payments to the business memberâs customerâs accounts. See Chapter 6, Enrolling in BPRP.
Financial institutions in other provinces, contact your provincial central for information on data file availability.
1.2 	BPRP Fees and Agreements
BPRP Service Fees
Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario, can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725.
All other financial institutions, contact your provincial central.
Other Financial Institution Fees
Other financial institutions participating in the BRPR service may charge additional fees and require the business member to complete additional documents and/or agreements. Details may be listed on the financial institutionâs website. For more information, contact the Central 1 Billers team at
billers@central1.com
.
Note:	If additional fees are charged by another financial institution, the business member must sign a pre-authorized payment agreement. Fees are charged directly to the business memberâs account.
Related Information
